ESP32.sourcing_note: Widely available in Lagos computer markets/online; recommend heatsink for continuous use.

Arduino_Uno.sourcing_note: Extremely common locally; rugged voltage regulator resists minor wiring mistakes.

LED.sourcing_note: Ubiquitous in hobby shops; always use with a 220Ω series resistor to prevent burn-out.

PIR_Sensor.sourcing_note: Commonly stocked; sensitive to RF noise, keep at least 10cm away from Wi-Fi antenna.

HC-SR04.sourcing_note: Widely available; requires 5V logic level conversion if used with 3.3V ESP32 pins.

SG90_Servo.sourcing_note: Commonly available; draws high peak current, supply power externally (not from GPIO).

Buzzer.sourcing_note: Cheap and ubiquitous; check active vs passive type before programming.

Relay_Coil.sourcing_note: Available locally; requires transistor driver and flyback diode to prevent board damage.

Relay_Module.sourcing_note: Widely available; includes onboard optocoupler isolation, safer for direct connection.

DC_Motor.sourcing_note: Common toy motor; draws massive current under load, do not connect directly to GPIO.

Motor_Driver.sourcing_note: Commonly stocked (L298N/ULN2003); essential for DC motors or heavy relay coils.

DHT11.sourcing_note: Available; slow 2s read intervals, good for basic ambient temperature monitoring.

Photoresistor.sourcing_note: Ubiquitous; requires a 10kΩ resistor to form a voltage divider for analog reading.

LCD_I2C.sourcing_note: Common Character LCD with pre-soldered I2C backpack; saves pins by using SDA/SCL.
